sourceMovie.title: Miss Congeniality
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Miss Congeniality works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ocean's Eight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Miss Congeniality clicked for you, Ocean's Eight (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Miss Congeniality clicked for you, Red Notice (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Miss Congeniality?
faq.answer: Try Ocean's Eight, Red Notice, Pain & Gain—each shares DNA with Miss Congeniality while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Miss Congeniality?
faq.answer: Miss Congeniality (2000) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Miss Congeniality part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Miss Congeniality Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
